2603.23171.title: Robust Safety Monitoring of Language Models via Activation Watermarking
2603.23171.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) can be misused to reveal sensitive information, such as weapon-making instructions or writing malware. LLM providers rely on $\emph{monitoring}$ to detect and flag unsafe behavior during inference. An open security challenge is $\emph{adaptive}$ adversaries who craft attacks that simultaneously (i) evade detection while (ii) eliciting unsafe behavior. Adaptive attackers are a major concern as LLM providers cannot patch their security mechanisms, since they are unaware of how their models are being misused. We cast $\emph{robust}$ LLM monitoring as a security game, where adversaries who know about the monitor try to extract sensitive information, while a provider must accurately detect these adversarial queries at low false positive rates. Our work (i) shows that existing LLM monitors are vulnerable to adaptive attackers and (ii) designs improved defenses through $\emph{activation watermarking}$ by carefully introducing uncertainty for the attacker during inference. We find that $\emph{activation watermarking}$ outperforms guard baselines by up to $52\%$ under adaptive attackers who know the monitoring algorithm but not the secret key.
2603.23171.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 可能被濫用來揭露敏感資訊，例如製造武器的指示或撰寫惡意軟體。LLM 提供者依賴 $\emph{監控}$ 來檢測和標記推理過程中的不安全行為。一個開放的安全挑戰是 $\emph{適應性}$ 對手，他們設計攻擊以同時 (i) 逃避檢測，同時 (ii) 引發不安全行為。適應性攻擊者是一個主要的擔憂，因為 LLM 提供者無法修補他們的安全機制，因為他們不知道他們的模型是如何被濫用的。我們將 $\emph{穩健}$ LLM 監控視為一場安全遊戲，其中了解監控的對手試圖提取敏感資訊，而提供者必須以低假陽性率準確檢測這些對抗查詢。我們的工作 (i) 顯示現有的 LLM 監控對適應性攻擊者是脆弱的，並且 (ii) 通過 $\emph{激活水印}$ 設計改進的防禦，通過在推理過程中仔細引入不確定性來對抗攻擊者。我們發現 $\emph{激活水印}$ 在了解監控算法但不知道秘密金鑰的適應性攻擊者下，性能比防護基線高出多達 $52\%$。
2603.23171.author: Toluwani Aremu et.al.
2603.23171.authors: Toluwani Aremu, Daniil Ognev, Samuele Poppi, Nils Lukas
